# PyWebVue

> Minimal Python + Vue bridge framework based on pywebview. Clone and develop with zero boilerplate.

## Overview

PyWebVue wraps pywebview with a clean bridge API for exposing Python methods to a Vue 3 frontend and pushing events back. The frontend is a Vite + Vue + TypeScript app. Designed as a "clone and develop" project.

## Architecture

```
Python (pywebview)                    Vue + TypeScript
  Bridge._window --js_api-->  window.pywebview.api
  Bridge._emit()    ----->    CustomEvent("pywebvue:{name}")
  @expose methods   <------   api.{method}(...args)
```

Three core components:

- **Bridge** -- base class with `_emit(event, data)` for Python->JS and `_on_drop()` for native file drag-and-drop
- **App** -- creates pywebview window, manages dev/prod URL resolution, starts event loop
- **@expose** -- decorator wrapping Bridge methods with try/except, returns `{success, error}` on failure

## Project Structure

```
main.py              App entry point (user edits this)
pywebvue/
  app.py             App class (window, dev/prod lifecycle, path resolution)
  bridge.py          Bridge base class + @expose decorator
  __init__.py        Exports: App, Bridge, expose
frontend/
  index.html         Vite entry point
  src/App.vue        Root Vue component (edit this)
  src/main.ts        Vue bootstrap
  src/bridge.ts      Bridge: call(), onEvent(), waitForPyWebView()
  src/env.d.ts       pywebview type declarations
dev.py               Dev startup (uv sync + npm install + Vite + Python app)
build.py             Packaging (PyInstaller desktop + Buildozer Android)
app.spec             PyInstaller spec with [MODIFY] sections
dev.bat / dev.sh     One-click launchers (cross-platform)
```

## Key Commands

```bash
# Development
uv run dev.py                          Start Vite + app (default)
uv run dev.py --no-vite                Load from disk (production preview)
uv run dev.py --frontend-dir ./my-app  Custom Vue project
uv run dev.py --setup                  Install deps only

# Building
cd frontend && npm run build           Build Vue app -> frontend_dist/
uv run build.py                        Desktop onedir
uv run build.py --onefile              Desktop single exe
uv run build.py --android              Android APK (macOS/Linux)
uv run build.py --clean                Remove build artifacts
```

## Response Convention

All Python<->JS communication uses a consistent envelope:
```json
{"success": true, "data": <any>}
{"success": false, "error": "description"}
```

## Python API

```python
from pywebvue import App, Bridge, expose

class MyApi(Bridge):
    @expose
    def my_method(self, arg: str) -> dict:
        return {"success": True, "data": f"got {arg}"}

api = MyApi()
app = App(api, title="My App", frontend_dir="frontend_dist", width=1024, height=768)
app.run()           # auto-detect: dev when not frozen
app.run(dev=False)  # force load from disk
app.emit("event", {"key": "val"})  # push to frontend
```

App.__init__ params: bridge, title, width, height, min_size, frontend_dir, dev_url
App.run params: dev (True/False/None), debug (True/False/None)
Bridge: _emit(event, data), get_dropped_files(), _on_drop(event)

## TypeScript API

Bridge functions in frontend/src/bridge.ts, imported directly in Vue components:
```ts
import { call, onEvent, waitForPyWebView, ApiResponse } from "./bridge"

await waitForPyWebView()
const res = await call<T>(method, ...args)
const off = onEvent<T>(name, (detail) => {})
```

## Dev vs Prod Path Resolution

- `dev=True`: loads URL from `dev_url` (default: `http://localhost:5173`)
- `dev=False`: loads `{frontend_dir}/index.html` from disk
- Auto-detect: `app.run()` -- dev=True when `sys.frozen` is False, False when frozen
- PyInstaller --onefile: `sys._MEIPASS / frontend_dir`
- PyInstaller --onedir: `sys.executable.parent / frontend_dir`

## Packaging Notes

- Desktop: PyInstaller via app.spec. Edit [MODIFY] sections for entry script, app name, icon, frontend assets.
- Android: Buildozer via build.py --android. Generates buildozer.spec on first run.
- GUI engines: Windows=EdgeWebView2/CEF, macOS=Cocoa WebKit, Linux=GTK WebKit
- Exclude unused frameworks (PyQt/PySide/tkinter) in app.spec to reduce size
- Android: single window only, no file dialogs, requires macOS or Linux

## Detailed Documentation

> - docs/development.md -- Dev modes, writing your app, frontend structure
> - docs/api.md -- Complete API reference for Python and TypeScript
> - docs/building.md -- PyInstaller config, Android/Buildozer setup, platform notes
